note:
进步团体“要求进步”(Demand Progress)和“进步数据”(Data For Progress)独家分享给Axios的一项最新民调显示，两党大约一半的美国人支持司法部针对谷歌的反垄断诉讼，而反对的不到三分之一。
重要原因：随着州和联邦反垄断行动包围谷歌和Facebook等公司，越来越多的证据表明，针对大型科技公司的监管行动得到了两党的支持。
虽然在内容审核、隐私和错误信息等科技政策问题上存在许多党派分歧，但认为科技太大、太强大的政策制定者和普通美国人比以往任何时候都多。
事实核查：赢得反垄断诉讼对政府来说是一个巨大的提升，通过新的反垄断立法是困难的。
数字：在10月24-25日由Demand Progress和Demand Progress Education Fund对979名可能的选民进行的在线调查中(误差率为+-3.1个百分点)，48%的人表示他们强烈或在一定程度上支持司法部的诉讼。32%的人强烈或或多或少地反对。
两党的数据相当一致，52%的共和党人支持这起诉讼，相比之下，49%的民主党人支持这一诉讼。26%的受访共和党人反对，32%的民主党人反对。
62%的受访者强烈或在一定程度上同意大型科技公司的经济实力是美国经济的问题；23%的受访者强烈或有点不同意。
当被问及潜在总统乔·拜登(Joe Biden)是否应该更多地打击大型科技公司，继续或结束司法部的诉讼时，49%的受访者表示，他应该走得更远。28%的人回应他应该结束诉讼。
54%的受访者强烈或在一定程度上同意拜登应该避免从谷歌和亚马逊等公司招聘人员进入他的政府；26%的受访者强烈或某种程度上不同意。
note_en:
About half of Americans on both sides of the aisle back the Justice Department's  antitrust lawsuit against Google, while fewer than a third oppose it, according to a new poll from progressive groups Demand Progress and Data for Progress shared exclusively with Axios.
 Why it matters: There's a growing pile of evidence that regulatory action against Big Tech has bipartisan support, as  state and federal antitrust action circles companies like Google and Facebook.
 While there are many party-line splits on tech policy issues like content moderation, privacy and misinformation, more policymakers and average Americans than ever agree tech is too big and powerful.
 Reality check: Winning antitrust suits represents a massive lift for the government and passing new antitrust legislation is hard.
 By the numbers: In an  online survey of 979 likely voters polled by Demand Progress and the Demand Progress Education Fund from October 24-25 (with a margin of error of +-3.1 percentage points), 48% said they strongly or somewhat support the DOJ's lawsuit. 32% strongly or somewhat oppose it.
 The numbers were fairly consistent across both parties, with 52% of Republicans supporting the suit, compared to 49% of Democrats. 26% of Republicans polled opposed it, while 32% of Democrats did.
 62% of respondents strongly or somewhat agree that the economic power of big tech companies is a problem for the U.S. economy; 23% strongly or somewhat disagree.
 Asked if a potential President Joe Biden should crack down on big technology companies more, continue the DOJ lawsuit or end it, 49% of respondents said he should go further. 28% responded he should end the lawsuit.
 54% of respondents strongly or somewhat agreed that Biden should avoid hiring into his administration people from companies like Google and Amazon; 26% strongly or somewhat disagreed.
title: 对979名可能的美国选民进行的调查发现，48%的人强烈或某种程度上支持美国司法部的谷歌诉讼，62%的人表示，大型科技公司的经济实力是美国经济的一个问题
title_en: Survey of 979 likely US voters finds 48% strongly or somewhat support DOJ's Google suit and 62% says Big Tech's economic power is a problem for US economy

note:
包括贝宝(PayPal)、条纹、Facebook和亚马逊(Amazon)在内的技术平台正在接受向美国仇恨团体支付的款项，尽管一些平台有明确的政策禁止使用它们为仇恨或暴力提供便利。
根据反极端主义智库战略对话研究所(Institute For Strategic Dialog)和非营利性研究组织全球虚假信息指数(Global Disinformation Index)的一项分析，美国全国广播公司新闻(NBC News)独家看到了这份分析报告，73个总部位于美国的仇恨团体已经获得了至少54个在线资助机制。10月3日之前，这些渠道包括众筹、电子商务、在线支付工具和加密货币。其中32个仇恨团体具有非营利性身份，这使得他们可以通过慈善筹款平台筹集资金。
其中21个团体使用贝宝(PayPal)筹集资金。19个人能够通过Facebook的捐赠工具筹集资金，13个人在他们的网站上使用条纹公司的技术来接受付款。13个团体通过Amazon.com出售商品或文学，其中包括白人民族主义团体VDARE的电子书选集。
研究人员呼吁科技平台采纳和执行限制仇恨团体使用它们的政策，并鼓励国会就美国国税局(IRS)是否应该给予歧视不可变特征的团体非营利地位展开辩论--特别是在冠状病毒大流行和选举之后。
战略对话研究所(Institute For Strategic Dialog)高级研究经理雅各布·戴维(Jacob Davey)说，我们在整个2020年都看到了线下和在线上仇恨的动员，这与流行病和竞选活动都有关。雅各布·戴维是这项研究的作者，他的研究题为“资助偏执：美国仇恨团体在线融资策略概览”(Finding Obotry：All of the Online Funding Strategy of American Hate Groups)。今年以来，许多团体动员了现实世界中的暴力行为，选举本身仍然是潜在敌意的脆弱时刻。
过去几年的大量分析研究了仇恨团体的资助机制，包括反诽谤联盟、媒体与民主中心、南方贫困法律中心和变化之色的报告。
哈佛大学(Harvard University)肖伦斯坦媒体、政治和公共政策中心(Shorenstein Center on Media，Politics and Public Policy)研究主任琼·多诺万(Joan Donovan)表示，仇恨言论和极端主义专家发现，将这些公司当作中立的基础设施提供商对待，是一种不再被社会容忍的借口。她说，虽然公民社会团体和记者可以从外部施加压力，但公司的科技工作者有能力从内部实施变革。
多诺万说，当为你工作的人不想为你工作时，你就无法维持一家企业。技术的广泛保护伞不会建造它，随着时间的推移，它将开始产生影响。当然，不幸的是我们不应该等待。
关于仇恨群体的最新研究集中在具有信仰或做法的群体，这些群体基于种族、宗教、性别、性取向或残疾等一成不变的特征攻击、诽谤、使某类人合法化或将其排除在外。
这73个群体是从南方贫困法律中心和反诽谤联盟创建的现有数据集中挑选出来的，并由研究人员进行额外的审查。要纳入分析，一个团体需要在2020年活跃起来，拥有可识别的成员基础，并开展可识别的活动，如创作传播仇恨意识形态的文学或其他内容，或参与街头仇恨动员。
研究人员将73个团体分为几个类别，包括白人至上主义者、白人民族主义者、反LGBTQ+、反穆斯林、反移民和街头抗议/民兵。
淘汰这些群体一直是贝宝(PayPal)的一个长期问题。该公司的可接受使用政策禁止利用其平台处理支付，以促进仇恨、暴力、种族或其他形式的歧视性不容忍。在南方贫困法律中心发现，2017年在弗吉尼亚州夏洛茨维尔组织联合右翼集会时，一名反抗议者被汽车撞死后，该公司一再承诺拒绝白人至上主义者和其他仇恨团体使用其支付和捐赠工具。
但研究人员发现，白人民族主义团体美国优先学生正在使用贝宝募集捐款。民兵组织或街头抗议团体，包括美国爱国者美国和美国革命2.0，使用贝宝接受捐款，爱国者祈祷创始人乔伊·吉布森用它为他的法律辩护基金筹集资金。吉布森面临2019年5月在俄勒冈州波特兰煽动骚乱的指控。
一些试图通过贝宝(PayPal)筹集资金的团体使用其他拼写或命名实体，显然是为了绕过贝宝(PayPal)的检测工具。例如，美国爱国者美国捐赠页面链接到一家名为Jack&amp；Jill Home Solutions；的公司的账户，但注明捐赠的目的是美国爱国者美国(原文拼写错误)。
贝宝发言人Kim Eichorn表示，公司正在审查研究中提到的账户。她说，贝宝团队对每个案例进行单独评估，并关闭违反公司可接受使用政策的账户。
艾霍恩说，贝宝致力于确保我们的服务不会被用来接受促进仇恨、暴力或种族不容忍活动的付款或捐赠。
研究人员发现，Facebook、Strip和亚马逊-所有这些公司都拒绝置评-尽管有一些政策来解决这个问题，但也面临着类似的挑战。在19个可以使用Facebook捐款工具(包括Facebook筹款活动)的仇恨团体中，有一个是VDARE，这是一个宣传白人民族主义观点的反移民组织，尽管Facebook在5月份删除了与该组织相关的页面。
反LGBTQ+的团体，如美国儿科医师学会(American College Of Pediatrics)和美国家庭协会(American Family Association)也可以在Facebook上筹集资金。美国儿科医师学会将同性恋与恋童癖联系起来，支持针对同性恋年轻人的所谓皈依疗法。美国家庭协会反对其创始人唐纳德·怀德蒙牧师(Rev.Donald Wildmon)所称的同性恋议程。
支付处理器Stiped为研究中的13个群体提供结账服务，这使得他们可以销售商品。其中包括VDARE和白人民族主义团体爱国者阵线(Patriot Front)和尼克·富恩特斯(Nick Fuentes)，“美国优先”(America First)。Stripe还为反穆斯林团体提供服务，其中包括ACT for America，该组织的创始人布里吉特·加布里尔(Brigitte Gabriel)曾表示，虔诚的穆斯林不能成为忠诚的美国公民。
包括白人至上主义团体盾墙网络(Shield Wall Network)和白人民族主义团体南方联盟(League Of The South)和VDARE在内的13个团体在亚马逊(Amazon.com)上出售物品-主要是意识形态文学。
这项研究归类为仇恨团体的许多组织在美国都有非营利性税收地位--报告的研究人员说，这个问题可能有助于仇恨团体合法化，并为他们提供筹集资金的途径。
事实上，Facebook似乎允许通过与筹款平台Network For Good的合作伙伴关系，向GuideStar数据库中拥有501(C)(3)状态的组织-总计约160万个团体-提供捐款。
同样，一个名为“慈善导航”(Charity Navigator)的平台允许向研究中确定的73个团体中的29个捐款，这也得到了“永远向善网络”(Network For Good)的推动，尽管该组织的首席公关官凯文·斯卡利(Kevin Scally)表示，在列出的团体中，只有9个通过该网站筹集了任何资金。
斯卡利说，“慈善导航者”并没有基于他们的使命而将非营利组织排除在外的政策，但这并不是不可能的。
战略对话研究所的戴维说：国会有必要首先就这些组织是否有资格获得非营利性税收地位进行辩论。它显然是一种机制，帮助他们合法化，并给人一种他们是在为公共利益行事的印象。
专家们响应了这份报告的呼吁，呼吁技术平台采取政策，禁止与仇恨团体做生意，或者更好地执行他们拥有的政策。
这类人一直存在于我们社会的边缘。品牌安全顾问南迪尼·贾米(Nandini Jammi)表示，他们之所以能够成长，只是因为他们得到了主流社交媒体和技术平台的帮助。南迪尼·贾米(Nandini Jammi)是一名品牌安全顾问，他开展的活动致力于让仇恨言论和虚假信息无利可图。没有这种支持，他们永远不可能获得过去十年来享有的突出地位和发展。
note_en:
Technology platforms including PayPal, Stripe, Facebook and Amazon are accepting payments to hate groups in the United States, even though some have explicit policies preventing their use to facilitate hate or violence.
 According to an analysis by the  Institute for Strategic Dialogue, an anti-extremism think tank, and the  Global Disinformation Index, a nonprofit research group, seen exclusively by NBC News, 73 U.S.-based hate groups have had access to at least 54 online funding mechanisms. The access includes crowdfunding, e-commerce, online payment tools and cryptocurrencies through Oct. 3. Thirty-two of the hate groups have nonprofit status, which allows them to raise money through charity fundraising platforms.
   Twenty-one of the groups used PayPal to raise money. Nineteen were able to raise funds through Facebook's donation tools, and 13 used Stripe's technology on their websites to receive payments. Thirteen groups were selling merchandise or literature, including e-book anthologies of articles by the white nationalist group VDARE through Amazon.com.
 The researchers called on tech platforms to adopt and enforce policies limiting their use by hate groups and to encourage congressional debate about whether the IRS should grant nonprofit status to groups that discriminate against "immutable" characteristics — especially in the wake of the coronavirus pandemic and the election.
 "We've seen offline and online mobilization of hate throughout 2020, both relating to the pandemic and to the election campaign," said Jacob Davey, senior research manager at the Institute for Strategic Dialogue and author of the research, titled " Bankrolling Bigotry: An Overview of the Online Funding Strategies of American Hate Groups." "Many groups have mobilized real-world violence over the course of this year, and the election itself remains a vulnerable moment for potential hostility."
 Numerous analyses over the last few years have examined the funding mechanisms of hate groups, including reports by the  Anti-Defamation League, the  Center for Media and Democracy, the  Southern Poverty Law Center and  Color of Change.
  Hate speech and extremism experts are finding that treating the companies like neutral providers of infrastructure is an "excuse that is no longer tolerated socially," said Joan Donovan, research director at Harvard University's Shorenstein Center on Media, Politics and Public Policy. She said that while civil society groups and journalists can apply pressure from the outside, tech workers at the companies have the power to effect change from within.
 "You can't maintain a business when the people who work for you don't want to work for you," Donovan said. "The broad umbrella of 'tech won't build it' will over time start to make an impact. What's unfortunate, of course, is we shouldn't have to wait."
 The latest research on hate groups focused on groups with beliefs or practices that attack, malign, delegitimize or exclude a class of people based on immutable characteristics, like ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation or disability.
 The 73 groups were selected from existing datasets created by the Southern Poverty Law Center and the Anti-Defamation League, with additional vetting by the researchers. To be included in the analysis, a group needed to be active in 2020, have an identifiable membership base and carry out identifiable activity, such as the creation of literature or other content spreading hateful ideology or involvement in street-based hate mobilization.
  Researchers divided the 73 groups into categories, including white supremacist, white nationalist, anti-LGBTQ+, anti-Muslim, anti-immigrant and street protest/militia.
   Weeding out the groups has been a perennial problem at PayPal. The company's  acceptable use policy prohibits the use of its platform to process payments to promote "hate, violence, racial or other forms of intolerance that is discriminatory." It has  made repeated pledges to deny white supremacist and other hate groups access to its payment and donation tools after the Southern Poverty Law Center found that it was "integral" in raising money to organize the "Unite the Right" rally in Charlottesville, Virginia, in 2017, at  which a counterprotester was hit by a car and killed.
 But researchers found that the white nationalist group America First Students is using PayPal to solicit donations. "Militia" or street protest groups, including American Patriots USA and American Revolution 2.0, use PayPal to receive donations, and Patriot Prayer founder Joey Gibson used it to raise money for his legal defense fund. Gibson faces  charges of inciting a riot in Portland, Oregon, in May 2019.
 Some groups attempting to raise money through PayPal use alternative spellings or named entities, apparently in an effort to circumvent PayPal's detection tools. For example, the American Patriots USA donation page links to an account in the name of a company called "Jack & Jill Home Solutions" but with a note stating that the purpose of the donation is "American Paatriots USA" (misspelling in the original).
  PayPal spokeswoman Kim Eichorn said the company was reviewing the accounts referred to in the research. She said a PayPal team assesses each case individually and closes accounts that violate the company's acceptable use policy.
 "PayPal is dedicated to ensuring that our services are not used to accept payments or donations for activities that promote hate, violence or racial intolerance," Eichorn said.
   Researchers found that Facebook, Stripe and Amazon — all of which declined to comment — have faced similar challenges even though they have some policies to address the problem. Among the 19 hate groups with access to Facebook's donation tools — including Facebook Fundraisers — is VDARE, an anti-immigration group that promotes white nationalist views, even though  Facebook removed pages associated with the group in May.
 Anti-LGBTQ+ groups such as the American College of Pediatricians, which links homosexuality to pedophilia and endorses so-called conversion therapy for gay young people, and the American Family Association, which  opposes what its founder, the Rev. Donald Wildmon, described as the "homosexual agenda," are also able to raise funds on Facebook.
  The payments processor Stripe provides checkout services for 13 of the groups featured in the research, which allows them to sell merchandise. Among them are VDARE and the white nationalist groups Patriot Front and Nick Fuentes' America First. Stripe also provides services for anti-Muslim groups, including ACT for America, whose founder, Brigitte Gabriel, has said practicing Muslims  "cannot be loyal citizens of the United States."
 Thirteen groups, including the white supremacist group Shield Wall Network and the white nationalist groups League of the South and VDARE, sold items — mostly ideological literature — on Amazon.com.
   Many of the organizations categorized by the research as hate groups have nonprofit tax status in the U.S. — a problem the report's researchers said "potentially helps legitimize hate groups and provides them with avenues through which to raise money."
 Indeed, Facebook appears to allow donations to nonprofit organizations featured in the GuideStar database of organizations with 501(c)(3) status — totaling about 1.6 million groups — through a partnership with the  fundraising platform Network for Good.
  Similarly, a platform called Charity Navigator allows donations to 29 of the 73 groups identified in the research, also facilitated by Network for Good, although the organization's chief relationship officer, Kevin Scally, said only nine of the listed groups had raised any money through the site.
 Scally said that Charity Navigator does not have a policy that excludes nonprofits based on their mission but that "it's not out of the question."
 Davey, of the Institute for Strategic Dialogue, said: "There is a need for congressional debate about whether such groups should qualify for nonprofit tax status in the first place. It clearly acts as a mechanism to help legitimize them and give the impression that they are acting in the public interest."
   Experts echoed the report's calls for technology platforms to adopt policies against doing business with hate groups or to better enforce the policies they have.
  "These types of people have always existed in the sidelines of our society. But they have only been able to grow because they've had the help of mainstream social media and technology platforms," said  Nandini Jammi, a brand safety consultant who runs campaigns dedicated to making hate speech and disinformation unprofitable. "Without that support, they'd never be able to gain the prominence and growth they have enjoyed through the past decade."
title: 研究：总部设在美国的仇恨团体正在使用条纹、PayPal、Facebook和亚马逊等平台筹集资金，尽管一些平台有反对仇恨团体的政策
title_en: Research: US-based hate groups are using platforms like Stripe, PayPal, Facebook, and Amazon to raise funds, even though some have policies against hate groups

note:
深谋远虑的首席执行官迪恩·尼古拉斯知道，你可能已经厌倦了听到聊天机器人。这款软件曾经承诺以更快的速度提供类似人类的客户服务，但由于令人摸不着头脑、死记硬背的回答和死胡同，这款软件失去了大部分炒作，这可能会让你梦想再次与人类交谈。
然而，在他成立三年的初创公司中，尼古拉斯表示，这些工具的构建方式与以往不同。他坚称，这些真的很有帮助。区别在于：真正的人工智能软件与基本的自动决策树。“我们知道你被烧伤了，”尼古拉斯说。“但那不是人工智能。与其说它是智能的，不如说是人工的。“。
总部位于旧金山的Business ForeThink提供了一种名为Agatha的工具，它认为这种工具实际上可以发挥作用，它为服务代理提供了一种产品，可以对支持票证进行分类，并使用自然语言处理和人工智能软件自行解决这些问题，随着时间的推移，人工智能软件在预测解决方案方面变得更加智能。该公司表示，到目前为止，他们已经自动解决了50多万起索赔。
投资者已经注意到：ForeThink周三宣布，它已在B轮融资中从之前的投资者NEA、Sound Ventures、Neo、Geodesic Capital和运营商Collective以及包括Qualtrics首席执行官瑞安·史密斯、肖恩·库姆斯和LL Cool J在内的高杠杆率个人那里筹集了1700万美元。
尼古拉斯曾是Dropbox和Pure Storage的工程师，2018年他与前LinkedIn工程师萨米·戈什(Sami Ghoche)共同推出了PreThink，两人在25岁和24岁时都出现在次年的福布斯30名30岁以下企业技术排行榜上。(另外三名团队成员也是该名单的校友。)。同年晚些时候，在由NEA牵头的首轮融资900万美元之前，PreThink赢得了TechCrunch的Disrupt Battlefield初创公司大赛。
让客户产生共鸣的是一款软件，它可以嵌入公司的销售和客户服务软件，以了解过去的问题是如何解决的，并更好地预测一张个人罚单可能是关于什么的。这些客户中，有名字的人也大多是科技行业的，比如Carta、Gusto和Masterclass。尼古拉斯说，这可以带来更高的准确性和更快的解决时间：“阿加莎能够通过倾听(人类)特工的反应，找出新问题何时开始出现，以及如何应对。”
在Sound Ventures，投资者兼演员阿什顿·库彻(Ashton Kutcher)表示，他最初对支持另一个客户服务工具持怀疑态度。深入挖掘，库彻说，声音团队对前瞻技术的真正工作方式印象深刻。库彻说：“这是我们投资组合中的许多公司都感到头疼的问题，因为随着一切变得全天候，客户服务必须是全天候的。当一切都是按需的，一切都在你的指尖上时，事情就会变得更加复杂。”
Qualtrics的史密斯补充说：“有很多人在研究不同的人工智能事物，但没有人真正有一个前门。”“通过观察Deon的所作所为，他们正在让每一种组织方式变得更加智能。”
就在乔治·弗洛伊德(George Floyd)被杀在全美掀起“黑人生命也是命”(Black Lives Matter)抗议活动之际，PreThink的第二轮融资说明书出炉了。对于尼古拉斯来说，结束这一轮融资是一段“艰难的时期”，他仍然是一位非常罕见的黑人初创公司创始人。在接下来的几周里，库彻和PreThink的投资者基础努力吸引著名的黑人企业家加入这轮融资，导致库姆斯和LL Cool J参与其中。
尼古拉斯说：“对我来说，讲述一个新的故事，改变成功的面貌真的很重要。”“我一直是一名建设者，所以如果这是我能为世界做的，那么我会这样做，在我工作的时候掀起波澜。”
note_en:
Forethought CEO Deon Nicholas knows you’re probably sick of hearing about chatbots. Once promised to provide human-like customer service at much faster speeds, the software’s lost much of its hype through head-scratching, rote answers and dead ends that can make you dream of speaking to a human being again.
  At his three-year-old startup, however, Nicholas says that the tools are built differently from the ground up. These ones, he insists, really help. The difference: real artificial intelligence software versus basic automated decision trees. “We know you’ve been burned,” Nicholas says. “But that wasn’t artificial intelligence. It was a lot more artificial than it was intelligent.”
 San Francisco-based business Forethought offers a tool called Agatha that it believes can actually work, with a product for service agents, triaging support tickets and resolving them on its own using natural language processing and AI software that gets smarter at anticipating solutions over time. The company says it’s resolved more than 500,000 claims automatically so far.
 Investors have noticed: Forethought announced on Wednesday it’s raised $17 million in a Series B funding round from previous investor NEA, Sound Ventures, Neo, Geodesic Capital and Operator Collective, alongside high-leverage individuals including Qualtrics CEO Ryan Smith, Sean “Diddy” Combs and LL Cool J.
 A former engineer at Dropbox and Pure Storage, Nicholas launched Forethought with ex-LinkedIn engineer Sami Ghoche in 2018, both appearing on the Forbes 30 Under 30  list for enterprise tech  for the following year at the ages of 25 and 24. (Three other team members are also alumni of the list.) Later that year, Forethought won TechCrunch’s Disrupt Battlefield startup contest before  raising a $9 million Series A led by NEA.
 What has made Forethought resonate with customers—nameable ones are mostly in tech, too, like Carta, Gusto and Masterclass—is software that plugs into a company’s sales and customer service software to learn how past issues were resolved and better predict what an individual ticket might be about. That can lead to much higher accuracy and faster resolution times, Nicholas says: “Agatha is able to figure out when new problems start to arise and how to respond to them by listening to how [human] agents are responding.”
 At Sound Ventures, investor and actor Ashton Kutcher says he was initially skeptical about backing another customer service tool. Digging in, Kutcher says the Sound team was impressed by how Forethought’s tech really worked. “It’s a headache so many of our portfolio companies have, because with everything becoming 24/7, customer service has to be 24/7. And it gets more complicated when everything is on-demand and everything is at your fingertips,” Kutcher says.
  “There’s a lot of folks who are working on different AI things, but no one really has a front door to it,” adds Qualtrics’ Smith. “Watching what Deon is doing, they are making every organization way more intelligent.”
 Term sheets for Forethought’s Series B arrived just as the killing of George Floyd set off Black Lives Matter protests across the U.S. That made closing the round “a tough time” for Nicholas, a still too-rare Black startup founder in tech. Over the following weeks, Kutcher and Forethought’s investor base worked to bring in prominent Black entrepreneurs to join the round, leading to the participation of Combs and LL Cool J.
 “For me, it is really important to tell a new story, change the face of what success looks like,” Nicholas says. “I’ve always been a builder, so if that’s what I can do for the world, then I’m going to do that and make waves while I’m at it.”
title: 制造人工智能客户支持软件的ForeThink筹集了1700万美元的B系列资金
title_en: Forethought, which makes AI-powered customer support software, raises $17M Series B

note: Media Matters对定期发布美国政治新闻的Facebook页面进行了一项为期九个月的新研究，研究了两种不同的表现指标，发现右倾页面的表现优于左倾页面，或者表现与之相似。右倾页面一直比左倾页面获得更多的平均每周互动，而两种类型的页面都获得了相似的参与率-这是一种衡量表现的指标，它不仅考虑了互动，还考虑了页面点赞和帖子的数量。主要发现包括：向右倾斜的页面比向左倾斜和未对齐的页面获得了更多的交互。在1月1日至9月30日期间，右倾的Facebook页面记录了超过60亿次互动(反应、评论、分享)，占发布美国政治新闻的页面总互动的43%，尽管只占帖子的26%。没有政治对齐的页面吸引的互动较少，尽管它们创建的帖子总数最多。我们这组互不一致的页面，其中许多来自传统媒体，发布了超过320万次--大约占总帖子的59%--但只占总互动的30%。向左倾斜的页面比向右倾斜或未对齐的页面更不活跃。这些页面发布的信息最少，点赞数量最少，获得的互动也最少。右倾和左倾页面的交互率相似，这是一种性能指标，衡量Facebook页面的参与度与页面点赞数量和分享帖子的频率有关。换句话说，尽管右倾页面总体上吸引了更多的互动，但在解释左倾页面较少的页面赞和帖子时，它们的表现与左倾页面相似。
note_en: A new nine-month Media Matters study of Facebook pages that regularly post about American political news looked at two different measures of performance and found that right-leaning pages outperformed left-leaning pages or performed similarly to them. Right-leaning pages consistently earned more average weekly interactions than left-leaning pages, while both types of pages earned similar engagement rates -- a measure of performance that accounts for interactions as well as numbers of page likes and posts.Key findings include:	Right-leaning pages earned more interactions than left-leaning and nonaligned pages. Between January 1 and September 30, right-leaning Facebook pages tallied more than 6 billion interactions (reactions, comments, shares), or 43% of total interactions earned by pages posting about American political news, despite accounting for only 26% of posts.	Pages without a political alignment drew fewer interactions even though they created the most total posts. Our group of nonaligned pages, many of them from traditional media outlets, posted more than 3.2 million times -- roughly 59% of total posts -- yet accounted for just 30% of total interactions.	Left-leaning pages were less active than right-leaning or nonaligned pages. These pages posted the least, had the fewest number of page likes, and earned the fewest interactions.	Right-leaning and left-leaning pages had similar interaction rates, a performance metric that measures engagement of a Facebook page in relation to the number of page likes and how frequently it shares posts. In other words, although right-leaning pages drew more interactions overall, they had similar performance to left-leaning pages when accounting for left-leaning pages' fewer page likes and posts.
title: 对1-9月政治新闻Facebook页面的研究：右倾页面获得60亿次互动，占总数的43%，而左倾页面获得26%的互动
title_en: Study of political news Facebook Pages from Jan.-Sept.: right-leaning Pages received 6B interactions, or 43% of the total, while left-leaning pages received 26%

note:
Robust.AI今天宣布，它已经筹集了1500万美元的首轮融资，由Jazz Venture Partners牵头。现有合作伙伴Playround Global，Liquid2，Fontinalis，Jaan Tallinn和Mark Leslie也参与了这轮融资，这使得这家总部位于旧金山湾区的机器人人工智能初创公司的融资高达2250万美元。
该公司成立于2019年年中，罗德尼·布鲁克斯(Rodney Brooks)是其C级高管之一。这位iRobot联合创始人担任这家初创公司的首席技术官，此前给世界带来了Baxter和Sawyer机器人的前景看好(但在财务上站不住脚)的反思出人意料地结束了。(另一位iRobot联合创始人海伦·格雷纳最近几个月也在一家新公司落地。)。与此同时，首席执行官加里·马库斯(Gary Marcus)也是几何智能(Geometry Intelligence)的联合创始人，该公司于2016年被优步(Uber)收购。
Robust.AI的核心是计划建立“世界上第一个用于机器人的工业级认知引擎”，实质上是为协作机器人提供足够的问题解决能力，使其能够有效地与人类并肩工作。
这家公司仍然是一家相当新的公司，但许多机器人和自动化投资似乎已经因为一场大流行而快速发展，这场大流行已经使大部分人类劳动力陷入瘫痪。Robust宣布的任务是彻底检查运行其中许多机器的软件堆栈，以便使它们在通常复杂的环境中更好地运行。
布鲁克斯在一份声明中说：“在机器人和人工智能系统中，找到市场契合度和其他任何产品一样重要。”“我们正在建造一些我们认为大多数机器人公司将无法抗拒的东西，将解决方案从今天可以在定义的环境中运行的单一用途工具，到可以在我们的世界及其所有错综复杂的环境中工作的高度有用的系统。”
note_en:
Robust.AI   today announced that it has raised a $15 million Series A, led by Jazz Venture Partners. Existing partners Playground Global, Liquid2, Fontinalis, Jaan Tallinn and Mark Leslie also participated in the round, which brings the Bay Area-based robotics AI startup’s funding up to $22.5 million.
 Founded mid-2019, the company counts Rodney Brooks among its C-level executives. The iRobot co-founder serves as the startup’s CTO, following the unexpected closure of the promising (but financially untenable) Rethink, which gave the world the Baxter and Sawyer robots. (Fellow iRobot co-founder Helen Greiner also  notably landed at a new venture in recent months). CEO Gary Marcus, meanwhile, is also the co-founder of  Geometric Intelligence, which was acquired by Uber, back in 2016.
 At the core of  Robust.AI are plans to build “the world’s first industrial-grade cognitive engine for robots,” essentially providing collaborative robots sufficient problem-solving capacity to effectively work alongside humans.
   The company is still quite new, but many robotics and automation investments have seemingly been fast-tracked by a pandemic that has hamstrung much of the human workforce. Robust’s stated mission is to overhaul the software stack that runs many of these machines, in order to to make them function better in often complex environments.
 “Finding market fit is as important in robots and AI systems as any other product,” Brooks said in a statement. “We are building something we believe most robotics companies will find irresistible, taking solutions from single-purpose tools that today  function in defined environments, to highly useful systems that can work within our world and all its intricacies.”
title: Robust.AI筹集1500万美元的首轮融资，由Jazz Venture Partners牵头，以改善协作机器人的问题解决
title_en: Robust.AI raises $15M Series A led by Jazz Venture Partners to improve problem solving for collaborative robots

note:
当亚马逊Prime Video用户在该平台上购买内容时，他们真正付费的是“无限期点播观看”的有限许可证，他们会在该公司的使用条款中被警告这一点。这就是该公司为什么应该驳回一起关于假设未来删除内容的诉讼的理由。
今年4月，阿曼达·考德尔(Amanda Caudel)起诉亚马逊不公平竞争和虚假广告。她声称，该公司秘密保留终端消费者访问通过其Prime Video服务购买的内容的权利。她代表自己和任何从2016年4月25日到现在从该服务购买视频内容的加州居民提起了可能的集体诉讼。
周一，亚马逊提出动议，驳回她的申诉，辩称她没有资格起诉，因为她没有受伤-并指出，自从提出申诉以来，她已经在Prime上购买了13本书。
原告声称，被告亚马逊的Prime Video服务，允许消费者购买视频内容用于流媒体或下载，误导了消费者，因为有时如果第三方权利持有者吊销或修改亚马逊的许可证，这些视频内容可能会变得不可用，律师大卫·比德曼在下面发布的动议中写道。起诉书含糊地指出了关于这一所谓的潜在危害的在线评论，但没有发现原告本人无法购买Prime Video的任何产品。事实上，原告购买的所有优质视频内容仍然可用。
此外，亚马逊认为，该网站要求的用户协议解释说，一些内容以后可能会变得不可用。
Biderman写道，这里最相关的协议-Prime Video使用条款-每次在亚马逊Prime Video上购买数字内容时都会呈现给消费者。这些使用条款明确规定，购买者仅获得观看视频内容的有限许可，并且由于提供商许可限制或其他原因，购买的内容可能变得不可用。&#34；
比德曼写道，个人不需要阅读协议就能受到它的约束。在线消费者交易中的商家服务条款协议在消费者对服务条款有合理通知的情况下是有效和可强制执行的。
title: 亚马逊提出动议驳回指控Prime Video购买虚假广告的诉讼，称其条款表明用户支付的是“有限许可”，而不是所有权
title_en: Amazon files motion to dismiss suit alleging false advertising of Prime Video purchases, says its terms indicate users pay for “limited license”, not ownership

note:
跳到主要内容如果您从Verge链接购买内容，Vox Media可能会赚取佣金。请参阅我们的道德声明。
亚马逊可能会因其更高端的Echo扬声器而获得更多关注，比如Echo Studio和新的第四代Echo，但不起眼的Echo Dot是目前阵容中最受欢迎的扬声器。2018年发布的第三代机型已被证明是一款出色的小智能音箱，凭借亚马逊频繁且咄咄逼人的销售，一首歌就能拿起一台，轻松将Alexa语音助手放入家中的每一个房间。
因此，在对第四代Echo进行大幅重新设计的同时，亚马逊也重新设计了Echo Dot也就不足为奇了。新的第四代圆点分享了它更大的兄弟姐妹的新球形设计，只是规模更小。它立即有三种不同的版本：标准型号售价49.99美元，内置LED时钟的版本售价59.99美元，以及可爱老虎或熊猫设计的儿童版售价59.99美元。
但是，虽然新的Echo在音质、功能和设计方面都有了全面的改进，但Echo Dot的改进幅度较小。在某些情况下，这是对先前模型的回归。
尽管如此，第四代Echo Dot仍然是一款出色的经济型智能扬声器，即使是全价。由于亚马逊的销量几乎不变，你很可能会以更低的价格买到一台。
这里最明显的更新是Echo Dot的新形状。就像全尺寸的Echo一样，新的Dot的形状就像一个失去了一些空气的球，所以它有一个平坦的侧面，可以防止它从架子上滚下来。球体的一半被布料覆盖，下半球是哑光塑料。它大约3.5英寸高，大约有垒球大小，是老式曲棍球设计的两倍高。(亚马逊的设计师似乎是受到运动器材的启发。)。
那个新设计是一把双刃剑。可以说，看起来更有趣，并动摇了人们对智能扬声器可能是什么样子的先入之见。它还提供稍好的声音性能，从相同的1.6英寸扬声器，安装在里面。
但新型号肯定没有上一代那么谨慎，而且它的尺寸更高，这意味着它更难安装在墙上。(当然，在亚马逊上)有一个完整的家庭式塑料支架行业，可以让你将较旧的Echo Dots直接安装到墙上的插座上，这是货架空间有限的房间的理想选择，比如浴室。但新型号的尺寸更大，这意味着做这件事将比以前尴尬得多。幸运的是，第三代机型似乎会停留一段时间，所以如果你计划安装，那是最合适的。
你可以得到三种不同颜色的标准新款Dot-深灰色、浅灰色和浅蓝色-尽管出于某种原因，这三种颜色都配有白色电源线，如果你购买较深的型号，这会很烦人。但是，如果您正在升级，则可以使用第三代型号的电源适配器。
就像在新的Echo上一样，Alexa正在收听时照亮的标志性LED灯环位于Echo Dot的底部，而不是以前型号的顶部。在实践中，这并没有太大的不同-我仍然可以从房间的另一边看到它，它在大多数表面上都有反射，所以它很亮。唯一的挑战是，当使用顶部的按钮调节音量时，可能很难看到光环。新的Dot还保留了3.5毫米的线路输出，可以让你将其连接到更大的扬声器上，尽管它不能作为其他音频源的线路输入，比如全尺寸Echo提供的音频来源。
在音质方面，新的Dot不如新的Echo有很大的飞跃。它比第三代机型听起来更好，但只是轻微的-你真的必须并排比较它们才能注意到不同之处。由于圆顶形状的设计，声音更饱满一些，音场也略有改善，但扬声器的大小和功率与以前一样，不像Echo那样支持立体声。您可以像以前一样将两个Dot配对成立体声配置，但不能跨代配对-您需要购买两个第四代型号才能设置立体声。新的Dot也没有更大型号的实时房间调谐功能，也没有杜比音频处理功能。
音质没有大的改善并不是一件坏事。第三代机型在价格和尺寸上听起来都好得令人震惊，而新机型也大同小异。它可以变得出人意料地响亮；对于音乐、有声读物、播客和与Alexa的互动来说，它都是令人愉快的；而且对于较小的房间来说，它是理想的选择。显然，它缺少的是低音响应，但除此之外，Echo Dot听起来非常棒，我更喜欢它，而不是谷歌类似价格的Nest Mini。当我们有机会测试时，我们将不得不看看苹果更昂贵但大小相似的HomePod mini相比有多好。不过，主要的结论是，你不应该从第三代开始升级，希望在音质上有一个重大的飞跃，它就是不在那里。
在新的Echo Dot变种中，我最喜欢的是内置LED显示屏的那款。它很适合做床头柜，你可以把它当做智能闹钟，通过语音编程。LED在默认情况下会显示时间，但也可以在您要求时显示当前的室外温度，或者在您调整音量时显示音量级别(从1到10)。只要有足够的信息，它就不会在卧室里分心，也不会像有摄像头的智能显示器那样增加隐私方面的顾虑。(当然，一直在听的麦克风带来的隐私问题仍然存在。)。你甚至可以轻触Dot的顶部来打盹，我发现这比联想的智能闹钟机型上的类似功能更容易做到这一点。(所有新款Echo都支持这一点，包括全尺寸Echo和标准的第四代Echo Dot。)。
儿童版Echo Dot是我见过的最可爱的智能扬声器，它的老虎或熊猫设计和垒球大小。它附带了亚马逊为期一年的Kids Plus服务(以前称为FreeTime UnLimited)，提供对儿童友好的有声读物、技能和游戏，以及一系列家长控制。亚马逊还将在购买后两年内免费更换儿童版，如果您的孩子决定用它扔掉、踢它或玩棒球。我个人并不倾向于在我孩子的卧室里放一个智能扬声器，但如果我愿意的话，新的儿童版Echo Dot将会是一个合适的选择。
Echo Dot仍然是我推荐的入门级智能扬声器。相对于它的价格和大小，它提供了出色的音质；可以使用Alexa能够提供的所有功能，包括播放各种流媒体服务的音乐、控制智能家居小工具、设置定时器和闹钟，甚至可以用作房间之间的对讲机；而且在大多数房间都很容易安装。它的价格也足够低，你可以购买多个Dot，将它们分散在你家各处，或者将它们配对成立体声配置，特别是当亚马逊(Amazon)进行频繁销售的时候。
但与之前的模型相比，我对回归的通用性并不满意。当然，由于新的设计，新版本的声音听起来确实稍微好了一些，但这种设计在放置新Dot的位置上做出了一些真正的妥协。虽然第四代Echo由于其球形设计在音质上有了巨大的飞跃，但从紧凑的圆柱体切换到笨拙的球体在这里并不能提供这些回报。这个形状对圆点来说就没什么意义了。
如果新的形状是你计划如何使用Dot的障碍，我建议你在旧型号完全售罄之前买下它。对于其他所有人来说，新的Echo Dot是可以买到的经济型智能扬声器。

    Amazon may get more attention for its higher-end Echo speakers, such as the Echo Studio and the new 4th Gen Echo, but the humble Echo Dot is by far the most popular speaker in the lineup. The  3rd Gen model, released in 2018, has proven to be an excellent little smart speaker, and with Amazon’s frequent and aggressive sales, you can pick one up for a song, making it easy to put the Alexa voice assistant in every room of your home.
 So it’s no surprise that alongside the  big redesign to the 4th Gen Echo, Amazon has also redesigned the Echo Dot. The new 4th Gen Dot shares the new spherical design of its larger sibling, just on a smaller scale. It’s immediately available in three different variations: the  standard model for $49.99, a version with  a built-in LED clock for $59.99, and a Kids Edition that comes in a cute  tiger or panda design for $59.99.
 But while the new Echo came with improvements across the board in terms of sound quality, features, and design, the Echo Dot’s improvements are smaller. And in some cases, it’s a regression from the prior model.
 Still, the 4th Gen Echo Dot remains an excellent budget smart speaker, even at its full price. And with Amazon’s near constant sales, you’ll likely be able to get one for even less.
   The most obvious update here is the Echo Dot’s new shape. Just like the full-size Echo, the new Dot is shaped like a ball that’s lost some air, so it has a flat side that prevents it from rolling off of a shelf. Half of the sphere is covered in fabric, with the bottom hemisphere a matte plastic. At about 3.5 inches tall, it’s about the size of a softball and twice as tall as the old, hockey puck design. (It appears that Amazon’s designers are inspired by sports equipment.)
 That new design is a double-edged sword. It’s arguably more interesting to look at and shakes up the preconceptions of what a smart speaker can look like. It also provides slightly better sound performance from the same 1.6-inch speaker that’s mounted inside.
  But the new model is definitely less discreet than the prior generation, and its taller size means it’s harder to mount on a wall. There’s a whole cottage industry of plastic mounts available (on Amazon, of course) that let you mount the older Echo Dots right to a wall outlet, which is ideal for rooms with limited shelf space, such as a bathroom. But the new model’s larger size means doing this will be much more awkward than before. Fortunately, it appears that the  3rd Gen model will be sticking around for a bit, so if you plan on mounting, that’s the one to go with.
  You can get the standard new Dot in three different colors — dark gray, light gray, and a light blue — though for some reason all three colors come with a white power cord, which is annoying if you buy the darker model. You can use the power adapter from the 3rd Gen model if you are upgrading, however.
  Like on the new Echo, the signature LED light ring that illuminates when Alexa is listening is on the bottom of the Echo Dot, as opposed to the top in prior models. In practice, this hasn’t made much of a difference — I can still see it from across the room and it reflects off of most surfaces, so it’s plenty bright. The only challenge is the light ring can be hard to see when adjusting the volume using the buttons on top. The new Dot also maintains the 3.5mm line out that lets you hook it up to a larger speaker, though it doesn’t function as a line in for other audio sources like the full-size Echo offers.
  In terms of sound quality, the new Dot is not as much of a leap forward as the new Echo. It is better-sounding than the 3rd Gen model, but only slightly — you really have to be comparing them side by side to notice the difference. The sound is a little more full and there is slightly better soundstage thanks to the dome-shaped design, but the speaker is the same size and power as before and it doesn’t support stereo like the Echo. You can pair two Dots into a stereo configuration, just like before, but you can’t do it across generations — you’ll need to buy two 4th Gen models for a stereo setup. The new Dot also doesn’t do the real-time room tuning of the larger model, nor does it have Dolby Audio processing.
 The fact that the sound isn’t greatly improved is not a bad thing. The 3rd Gen model sounded shockingly good for its price and size, and the new model is much the same. It can get surprisingly loud; is pleasant to listen to for music, audiobooks, podcasts, and interacting with Alexa; and is ideal for smaller rooms. The obvious thing it’s lacking is bass response, but otherwise the Echo Dot sounds fantastic for what it is and I prefer it over Google’s similarly priced Nest Mini. We’ll have to see how well Apple’s more expensive but similarly sized HomePod mini compares when we get a chance to test it. The main takeaway, though, is that you shouldn’t upgrade from the 3rd Gen hoping for a significant leap in sound quality, it’s just not there.
   Of the new Echo Dot variations available, my favorite is the one that comes with the LED display built into it. It’s great for a nightstand, where you can use it as a smart alarm clock that you program by voice. The LEDs show the time by default, but they can also display the current outside temperature when you ask for it, or show the volume level from one to ten as you adjust it. It’s just enough information that it isn’t distracting in a bedroom and it doesn’t come with the added privacy concerns of a smart display that has a camera. (The privacy concerns that come with an always-listening microphone remain, of course.) You can even tap the top of the Dot to snooze the alarm, which I find easier to do on this than with the similar feature on Lenovo’s Smart Alarm Clock models. (All of the new Echo models support this, including the full-size Echo and the standard 4th Gen Echo Dot.)
 The Kids Edition Echo Dot is by far the cutest smart speaker I’ve ever seen, with its tiger or panda designs and softball size. It comes with a year of Amazon’s Kids Plus service (formerly known as FreeTime Unlimited) that provides access to kid-friendly audiobooks, skills, and games, as well as an array of parental controls. Amazon will also replace the Kids Edition for free within two years of purchase if your kid decides to drop kick it or play baseball with it. I personally am not inclined to put a smart speaker in my kids’ bedrooms, but if I were to, the new Kids Edition Echo Dot would be the one to do it with.
  The Echo Dot remains my recommendation for an entry-level smart speaker. It provides excellent sound quality relative to its price and size; has access to all of the things Alexa is capable of, including playing music from a variety of streaming services, controlling smart home gadgets, setting timers and alarms, and even functioning as an intercom between rooms; and is easy to stick in most rooms. It’s also priced low enough that you can purchase multiple Dots and scatter them throughout your home or pair them in stereo configurations, especially when Amazon runs one of its frequent sales.
  But I’m not happy with the regression in versatility compared to the prior model. Sure, the new version does sound slightly better thanks to its new design, but that design comes with some real compromises in where you can put the new Dot. While the 4th Gen Echo saw massive leaps in sound quality thanks to its spherical design, switching from a compact cylinder to an awkward orb doesn’t provide those returns here. This shape just makes less sense for the Dot.
 If the new shape is a road block for how you plan to use the Dot, I’d recommend picking up the older model before it fully sells out. For everyone else, the new Echo Dot is the budget smart speaker to get.

title: 亚马逊Echo Dot(2020)评论：音质出人意料的好，麦克风不错，价格低廉，但新的球形设计更显眼，也更难安装
title_en: Amazon Echo Dot (2020) review: surprisingly good sound quality with nice mics at a low price, but new spherical design is more conspicuous and hard to mount

note:
跳到主要内容如果您从Verge链接购买内容，Vox Media可能会赚取佣金。请参阅我们的道德声明。
LG翼是一款不同于其他任何一款智能手机的智能手机，其疯狂的旋转设计使其在与2020年最古怪的手机相比也是脱颖而出的。这也是LG新的Explorer Project品牌的第一款手机，该品牌希望试验新的方式来思考我们如何设计和使用我们的智能手机。
售价999美元的机翼是一个大胆的尝试，但它无法将这种创造力转化为一款物有所值的手机。
LG翼看起来很酷，但也很贵-999美元，它的价格与全新的iPhone 12 Pro或Galaxy S20一样高，而且比其他许多神奇的手机都要贵。这是一大笔钱花在一个时髦的屏幕噱头上，这最终是使wing与类似规格的较便宜手机区别开来的最大因素，比如摩托罗拉One 5G，Pixel 4A和Pixel 5，以及LG今年早些时候推出的自己的天鹅绒手机。
看着机翼，很容易把它误认为是另一款超大尺寸的Android手机。事实上，它看起来很像LG天鹅绒手机，这款手机也分享了它的大部分规格。采用6.8英寸、9：20.5的OLED显示屏，比Galaxy S20 Ultra略小、略厚。归功于LG的设计：在0.43英寸(10.9毫米)厚的情况下，它仍然足够紧凑，你不一定会猜到还有其他东西。(事实上，我试着询问了几个家庭成员，他们都没有怀疑有什么不寻常的地方。)。
当然，机翼不仅仅是一部大的安卓手机。只需轻触手机的右下角，整个主显示屏就会向上旋转，并在屏幕下方露出第二块3.9英寸的OLED屏幕。
它的机械装置是一流的，显示屏平稳地滑动，并发出令人难以置信的令人满意的、可听见的“咔嗒”声。点击这里查看我们的实际操作视频，以便更好地了解所有这些内容的外观和听起来是什么样子。(顺便说一句，机翼只有在你用右手握住的情况下才能有效地打开，这对一些人来说可能是问题，也可能不是问题。)。
机翼背后的实际工程令人着迷，包括巧妙的机械技巧和一个微型液压阻尼器-JerryRigEverything的拆卸很好地了解了它是如何工作的。当你滑动打开手机时，你实际上可以感觉到减震器的踢动-当显示屏被减震器卡住时，会有非常小的半秒暂停，然后减震器缓慢而温和地完成旋转。
虽然硬件很棒，但机翼透露，像旋转和辅助显示器一样酷的概念，只是没有足够的实际使用案例来证明它们的合理性。
在Android上水平运行的应用程序并不多。大多数手机应用程序都被设计成在肖像模式下使用，就像大多数人大部分时间拿着大多数手机的方式一样。即使是具有横向模式的应用程序(如Twitter、Gmail，甚至Chrome)看起来也不是很好。
从技术上讲，你可以旋转手机，这样主显示屏就是垂直的，较小的屏幕像一个小残肢一样伸出，但这样握住机翼很尴尬，因为手机的大部分重量仍然几乎完全在现在水平的后部。
最好的用例是视频或游戏-这些任务实际上受益于水平方向，并且可以拥有潜在有用的第二屏幕应用程序。但在日常使用中，我发现花额外的时间和精力摆弄第二个屏幕模式比在应用程序之间来回切换更快或更值得。当然，你可以同时看YouTube视频和发短信，但购买手机似乎没有必要，因为你可能只需要买几次手机。
如果你愿意坐下来走自己的路，你可以想出一些用例，说明按照LG想象的方式使用机翼实际上是有意义的。例如，对于完成堡垒之夜每周的挑战来说，这是一个非常棒的设备：我可以在主屏幕上玩游戏，同时打开Chrome窗口或YouTube漫游，以显示我需要收集的特定物品或必须完成的目标的位置。
如果你在看电影时注意力持续时间很长，也可以在Twitter上滚动或查看IMDB，以找出这位演员在看Netflix时在看什么电视节目，尽管第二个小显示屏很难用来完成任何过于复杂的任务，因为它的尺寸和近乎正方形的宽高比。
在机翼上使用软件总是有一种笨拙的感觉。虽然一些应用程序-如LG的内置笔记软件或音乐应用程序-被设计为充分利用双显示器，但第三方应用程序的支持却很少见。很容易想象一个更多的应用程序可以利用第二个显示屏的世界：游戏可以为地图或库存提供Wii U型的第二个屏幕体验，或者地图可以在你的主导航一侧显示即将到来的方向列表。但是支持很少(在我尝试的所有游戏中，只有Asphalt9有真正的第二屏支持)。
例如，当你在第二个显示屏上观看视频时，YouTube会弹出一组有用的媒体控件，但几乎所有其他视频应用都不提供类似的选项。这也令人遗憾，因为屏幕旋转出去时覆盖了侧面的音量键，考虑到机翼的大小，以及它们与电源按钮(位于正下方)的大小和形状相同，它们已经很难找到了。
另一个障碍是机翼使用显示屏内的指纹读取器，它位于主显示屏的底部(你会认为它会在那里)。但使用机翼的自然方式并不是轻触屏幕并解锁指纹读取器，而是打开第二个显示屏，这将使指纹读取器不可能触手可及。我先在另一只手上注册了一个手指来解锁手机，然后就干脆把它关掉了。LG似乎意识到这是一个问题：当手机旋转打开时，用于手动输入密码的数字键盘会出现在你拇指下的小屏幕上，但由于没有在辅助屏幕上增加第二个显示屏内扫描仪，我不确定这是一个好的解决方案。
机翼的其余部分几乎检查了每一个盒子，看是否有中高端Android智能手机。骁龙765G加上8 GB的RAM，几乎足以流畅地运行所有东西(尽管仍然偶尔会出现滞后，特别是在旋转打开显示器时)。4000mAh的电池足以轻松维持一整天的电池寿命，即使大量使用这两个显示器也是如此。
LG翼也支持5G；我测试的机型是Verizon变体，它同时支持毫米波(Mmwave)和6 GHz以下的连接(包括Verizon新推出的全国网络)。该网络的速度略好于LTE，但真正的好处来自Verizon的mmWave网络，该网络仍然很稀缺。就像任何其他5G手机一样，除非你直接住在mmWave热点的顶部，否则这不是一个特别需要购买机翼的好理由。
1000美元的手机也有一些缺失：两个显示屏的屏幕刷新率都被限制在60 Hz，这可能会导致手机偶尔变慢(特别是考虑到这个价位范围内几乎所有其他Android手机都提供更好的价格)。这款手机没有防水功能，尽管无可否认，很难想象LG是如何通过旋转设计做到这一点的。
LG机翼有四个摄像头：顶框内置一个3200万像素的弹出式自拍摄像头，背面有三个后置摄像头。弹出式相机也不仅仅是消除凹槽的一种风格选择。即使在旋转显示器时，也必须将相机放在应有的位置。
不过，手机背面的情况更有趣，LG有三个摄像头：6400万像素的主摄像头，1300万像素的标准超宽摄像头，用于定期拿着手机，以及专门的1200万像素的超宽摄像头，其特点是物理旋转传感器专用于机翼的“万向节模式”。
显示器的性质意味着，当屏幕垂直旋转时，你只能使用两个标准摄像头，而当屏幕水平旋转时，你只能使用万向模式摄像头(LG表示，该摄像头有一个特殊的“六动”传感器用于稳定)。
主相机很好，色彩鲜艳，细节效果很好。但低光性能无法与2020年最好的手机相提并论，虽然LG的夜间模式有助于缓解这一问题，但它以额外的细节损失换取更明亮的拍摄。
但最值得注意的摄像头功能是万向节模式，它利用了机翼的设计：主显示屏显示您的视频输出，而较小的面板用作手柄，并配有万向节控件。但是，尽管它看起来像一个真正的万向架，LG的实现几乎完全是基于软件的-相机不像实际的万向架那样绕着不同的轴旋转以保持到位。它只是在宽广的视野中使用数字裁剪。
尽管如此，该软件的效果仍然令人印象深刻：LG在保持镜头锁定和稳定方面做得很好，即使是在相当大的运动量的情况下也是如此。在帧周围平移也会产生平滑、干净的镜头。但事实上，它完全是通过软件完成的，这意味着看起来最独特的功能实际上是LG可以在任何设备上复制的东西，如果它愿意的话。考虑到机翼的昂贵成本，很难想象你在这里能获得比Pixel5和电动智能手机万向节等更便宜的组合更好的体验。
就像机翼的其他部分一样，摄像头也不错--但就手机的价格而言，你可以花更多的钱让它变得更好。
我在“机翼”工作的那段时间，有一款应用非常适合它的独特设计：现在注定要倒闭的流媒体服务Quibi，它的类似花哨的“Turnstyle”视频格式(它的设计目的是在肖像和横向剪辑之间无缝切换)实际上是为手机量身定做的，它可以立即旋转显示屏来实现同样的功能。
但不幸的是，感觉LG翼-至少在目前的化身-注定了与Quibi相同的命运，后者的产品以更少的钱提供了更多、更好的服务。
LG机翼的旋转显示屏是工程上的奇迹，也是一种令人难以置信的有趣的新奇事物，这种设计创新在一个大多数智能手机设计都只是相同的黑色平板上的变体的世界里看到是令人兴奋的。但新奇有趣并不意味着好东西，如果没有更好的软件支持或更低的价格，除了最疲惫的智能手机用户之外，很难向任何人推荐机翼。

    The LG Wing is a smartphone unlike any other, with a wild rotating design that makes it stand out when compared to even the weirdest 2020 phones. It’s also the first phone in LG’s new Explorer Project brand, which looks to experiment with new ways to think about how we design and use our smartphones.
 The $999 Wing is a bold experiment, but it can’t translate that creativity into a phone that’s worth the added price.
  The LG Wing looks cool, but it’s also expensive — at $999, it costs as much as a brand-new  iPhone 12 Pro or  Galaxy S20 and more than plenty of other fantastic phones. That’s a lot of money to put down on a funky screen gimmick, which ultimately is the biggest factor that separates the Wing from less expensive phones with similar specs, like the  Motorola One 5G, the  Pixel 4A and  Pixel 5, and  LG’s own Velvet from earlier this year.
   Looking at the Wing, it’s easy to mistake it for just another supersized Android phone. In fact, it looks similar to the LG Velvet, a phone with which it also shares most of its specs. With a 6.8-inch, 9:20.5 OLED display, it’s just a bit smaller and a bit thicker than a Galaxy S20 Ultra. Credit to LG’s design here: at 0.43 inches (10.9mm) thick, it’s still compact enough that you wouldn’t necessarily guess that there’s anything more to it. (I tried, in fact, with several family members, none of whom suspected anything out of the ordinary.)
 Of course, the Wing isn’t just a big Android phone. With just a flick of the lower right corner of the phone, the entire main display swivels up and around to a horizontal orientation, revealing a second, 3.9-inch OLED screen underneath.
  The mechanism is top-notch, with the display gliding smoothly and snapping shut with an incredibly satisfying, audible “snick.”  Check out our hands-on video here for a better idea of what that all looks and sounds like. (As an aside, the Wing can effectively only be opened if you hold it in your right hand, which may or may not be an issue for some people.)
 The  actual engineering behind the Wing is fascinating, involving clever mechanical tricks and a miniature hydraulic damper —  JerryRigEverything’s teardown has a good look at how it all works. And you can actually  feel that damper kick in when you slide open the phone — there’s an ever-so-small half-second pause as the display gets caught on the damper, which then slowly and gently finishes its rotation.
 While the hardware is excellent, the Wing reveals that as cool of a concept as the rotating and secondary displays are, there are just not enough practical use cases to justify them.
  There aren’t that many applications that it makes sense to run in a horizontal orientation on Android. Most phone apps are designed to be used in portrait, the way that most people hold most phones most of the time. And even apps that do have landscape modes (like Twitter, Gmail, or even Chrome) just don’t look very good that way.
 Technically, you can use the phone rotated so the main display is vertical, with the smaller screen sticking out like a little vestigial limb, but it’s awkward to hold the Wing that way, as the bulk of the weight of the phone is still almost entirely in the now-horizontal back portion.
 The best use case is for video or gaming — tasks that actually benefit from a horizontal orientation and can have potentially useful second-screen apps. But there were so few situations in day-to-day use where I found it to be faster or worth the extra time and effort to fiddle around with the second screen mode instead of just switching back and forth between apps. Sure, you  can watch a YouTube video and text at the same time, but buying a phone just for the vanishingly few times that you might need to do that seems unnecessary.
  If you’re willing to sit down and go out of your way, you can come up with a few use cases where it actually makes sense to use the Wing the way LG imagines. It’s a fantastic device for completing weekly challenges in  Fortnite, for example: I could play the game on the main screen while keeping a Chrome window or YouTube walkthrough open to show the location of the specific items I needed to collect or objectives that I had to accomplish.
 If you have a terrible attention span during movies, it’s also good for scrolling through Twitter or checking IMDb to figure out what TV show that actor was in while watching Netflix, although the second small display is hard to use for any overly complex tasks due to its size and nearly square aspect ratio.
  There’s a constant awkwardness to using the software on the Wing. While a few apps — like LG’s built-in note-taking software or music apps — are designed to take full advantage of the dual display, support is rare on third-party apps. It’s easy to imagine a world where more apps could take advantage of a second display: games could offer a Wii U-esque second screen experience for maps or inventory, or Maps could display a list of upcoming directions over on the side of your main navigation. But support is few and far between (of all the games I tried, only  Asphalt 9 had any real second-screen support).
 YouTube, for example, will pop up a useful set of media controls when you’re watching a video on the second display, but nearly every other video app doesn’t offer similar options. It’s a shame, too, because the screen covers the volume keys on the side when rotated out, and they’re already hard enough to find given the size of the Wing and the fact that they’re the same size and shape as the power button (which is located directly below).
 Another barrier is the Wing’s use of an in-display fingerprint reader, which is located on the bottom of the main display (where you’d think it would be). But the natural way to use the Wing isn’t to tap the screen and unlock the fingerprint reader; it’s to swing open the second display, which puts the fingerprint reader impossibly out of reach. I first registered a finger on my other hand to unlock the phone, and then just shut it off altogether. LG seems aware that it’s an issue: when the phone is swiveled open, the numpad for manually entering a password appears on the smaller screen under your thumb, but short of adding a second in-display scanner on the secondary screen, I’m not sure what a good solution here is.
  The rest of the Wing checks nearly every box for an upper midrange Android smartphone. The Snapdragon 765G, combined with 8GB of RAM, is enough for smoothly running nearly everything (although there’s still the occasional spot of lag, particularly when it comes to rotating open the display). And the 4,000mAh battery is enough to easily last a full day of battery life, even with heavy use of both displays.
 The LG Wing also has support for 5G; the model I tested was the Verizon variant, which offers support for both millimeter wave (mmWave) and sub-6GHz connections (including Verizon’s newly launched nationwide network). Speeds on that network were marginally better than LTE, but the real benefit comes from Verizon’s mmWave network, which is still scarce. Like any other 5G phone, unless you live directly on top of a mmWave hotspot, it’s not a great reason to get the Wing specifically.
 There are also a few misses for a $1,000 phone: the screen refresh rate is limited to just 60Hz on both displays, which can help contribute to the occasional slowness of the phone (especially considering that nearly every other Android phone in this price range offers better). There’s no waterproofing, although it’s admittedly hard to imagine how LG could have accomplished that with the swiveling design.
   The LG Wing has four cameras: a single 32MP pop-up selfie camera built into the top frame and a trio of rear cameras on the back. The pop-up camera isn’t just a style choice to eliminate the notch, either. It’s necessary to keep the camera located where it should be, even when you’re rotating the display.
 Things are more interesting on the back of the phone, though, where LG has three cameras: a 64-megapixel main camera, a standard 13-megapixel ultrawide for use when holding the phone regularly, and a dedicated 12-megapixel ultrawide camera that features a physically rotated sensor that’s dedicated to the Wing’s “gimbal mode.”
  The nature of the display means that you can only use the two standard cameras when the screen is rotated vertically and the gimbal mode camera (which LG says has a special “hexa motion” sensor for stabilization) when the screen is rotated horizontally.
 The main camera is good, with bright colors and a good level of detail. But low-light performance isn’t on par with 2020’s best phones, and while LG’s night mode helps to alleviate that, it comes at an additional loss of detail in exchange for the brighter shot.
   But the most notable camera feature is the gimbal mode, which takes advantage of the Wing’s design: the main display shows your video output, while the smaller panel works as the grip, complete with gimbal controls. But as much as it looks like a real gimbal mount, LG’s implementation is almost entirely software-based — the camera isn’t rotating around on a different axis to keep in place, like an actual gimbal mount would. It’s just using digital cropping on the wide field of view.
  Still, the software effect is impressive: LG does a good job of keeping footage locked and stable, even through a fair amount of motion. Panning around a frame also results in smooth, clean footage. But the fact that it’s entirely done through software means that what looks like the Wing’s most unique feature is actually something that LG could replicate on any device, if it chose. And given the Wing’s already pricey cost, it’s hard to imagine that you’re getting a better experience here than with a cheaper combination of say, a Pixel 5 and  a motorized smartphone gimbal.
 Like the rest of the Wing, the cameras aren’t bad — but for what the phone costs, you can get better for your money.
  In my time with the Wing, there was one app that was perfectly suited to its unique design: the now-doomed streaming service,  Quibi, whose similarly  gimmicky “Turnstyle” video format (which is designed to seamlessly swap between portrait and landscape versions of a clip) was practically made for a phone that can instantly rotate its display to do the same.
 But unfortunately, it feels like the LG Wing — at least in its current incarnation — is set for the same fate as Quibi, outclassed by products that offer more and better service for less money.
 The LG Wing’s rotating display is a marvel of engineering and an incredibly fun novelty, the kind of design innovation that’s exciting to see in a world where most smartphone designs are just variations on the same identical black slabs. But being new and interesting doesn’t mean that something is good, and without better software support or a lower price tag, it’s hard to recommend the Wing for anyone but the most jaded smartphone owners.

title: LG翼评论：有趣的设计，出色的制造质量，坚固的性能，但是价格昂贵，它的折叠式第二屏没有太多用途
title_en: LG Wing review: interesting design, excellent build quality, and solid performance, but pricey and there are not many uses for its fold-out second display

普鲁米筹集了由NEA牵头的3750万美元的新投资，这代表着这家历史悠久的风险投资公司对这家西雅图初创公司用于编程云基础设施的软件开发工具投下了信任票。
NEA的执行普通合伙人斯科特·桑德尔周三上午在宣布这笔投资的一份声明中表示，这家由35人组成的初创公司于2017年由前微软工程主管乔·达菲和埃里克·鲁德创立，它已经开发出“多年来我们看到的最重要、最雄心勃勃的方法之一，来重新定义云的使用方式，就像过去的操作系统革命一样。”
总部位于西雅图的Madrona Venture Group和托拉资本(Tola Capital)两年前领导了普鲁米1500万美元的首轮A轮融资，也参与了新一轮的B轮融资。
NEA的正式名称是New Enterprise Associates，成立于1977年，在硅谷、波士顿、纽约和马里兰州等地区设有办事处。今年3月，该公司完成了一只36亿美元的基金，使其一生中筹集的总资本达到近240亿美元。其投资组合包括Cloudflare等其他云基础设施，以及医疗保健和机器人等领域的一系列初创企业。
这笔投资使普鲁米的总资金筹集到5750万美元。通过这轮融资，NEA合伙人亚伦·雅各布森(Aaron Jacobson)将与马德罗纳风险投资集团(Madrona Venture Group)董事总经理S·索马塞加(S.Somasegar)和托拉资本(Tola Capital)董事总经理希拉·古拉蒂(Sheila Gulati)一起加入Pulumi董事会。
普鲁米表示，将利用这笔资金加快研发，并扩大销售和营销举措。Jay Wampold是一位经验丰富的营销主管，曾在Chef、亚马逊网络服务(Amazon Web Services)、Qumulo和Cloudability等公司任职，最近加入普鲁米担任首席营销官。该公司表示，它还聘请了林赛·马罗里奇(Lindsay Marolich)担任需求产生高级总监，凯文·科特基(Kevin Kotecki)担任销售副总裁。
最近聘用的其他人包括陈立明(Lee-ming Zen)担任工程副总裁，建立了一个由首席技术官卢克·霍班(Luke Hoban)领导的技术团队。
这家公司面临着不乏的竞争。它正在与一系列被称为基础设施即代码的领域的备受瞩目的替代方案展开竞争，包括AWS CloudForment、Hashicorp Terraform等。
普鲁米表示，其今年的增长速度有所加快，部分原因是客户在大流行期间更加关注新的云项目。
“显然，人们正在加速向云计算转移，”普鲁米执行主席兼联合创始人鲁德(Rudder)说。“面对危机，他们的优先顺序更好了。那些可能被搁置的项目现在突然变成了实现云计算的当务之急。这无疑加速了整体进展。人们正在将越来越大的投资组合转移到云端。“。
Pulumi允许其客户将现代软件开发实践应用于传统上配置、配置和更新运行应用程序的底层硬件和软件的繁琐或手动过程。它的工具可以跨平台和技术运行，包括AWS、Microsoft Azure、Google Cloud、Kubernetes和其他，让开发人员可以使用包括JavaScript、TypeScript、Python和Go在内的通用语言，而不是学习专用和单一用途的语言。
其客户范围从小型初创公司到梅赛德斯-奔驰(Mercedes-Benz)、Tableau、DocuSign、VMware和Snowflake等大公司。Pulumi基于开源模式，个人用户可以免费使用社区版。它以软件即服务的模式销售多用户和企业计划。
该公司表示，现在每月其软件工具的下载量超过100万次，去年整体采用率增加了10倍。10月份，超过3000名与会者注册参加了普鲁米的云工程峰会。
“我们看到了巨大的增长，无论是在开源社区，还是在客户基础上，”该公司的联合创始人兼首席执行官达菲在接受采访时说。
Madrona Venture Group的Somasegar表示，他看到了推动Pulumi增长的两个趋势：将云置于软件开发中心的行业转变；以及对开发人员、运营专家、安全工程师和其他人在设计、开发、部署、监控和更新的连续循环中无缝合作的日益增长的需求。
索马塞加说：“这两个长期趋势是我一开始对普鲁米感到兴奋的地方，三年半后，这两个趋势继续保持着超级强劲的势头。”
note_en:
Pulumi has raised a new $37.5 million investment led by  NEA, representing a vote of confidence by the longtime venture capital firm in the Seattle startup’s software development tools for programming the underlying infrastructure of the cloud.
 The 35-person startup, founded in 2017 by  former Microsoft engineering leaders Joe Duffy and Eric Rudder, has developed “one of the most significant and ambitious approaches to redefining how the cloud is used that we’ve seen in years, much like operating system revolutions of the past,” said Scott Sandell, NEA’s managing general partner, in a statement Wednesday morning announcing the investment.
 Seattle-based  Madrona Venture Group and  Tola Capital, which  led Pulumi’s $15 million Series A funding round two years ago, also participated in the new Series B funding round.
   NEA, formally known as New Enterprise Associates, was founded in 1977 and has offices in areas including Silicon Valley, Boston, New York and Maryland. It  closed on a $3.6 billion fund in March, bringing the total capital raised over its lifetime to nearly $24 billion.  Its portfolio includes other cloud infrastructure such as Cloudflare, as well as an array of startups in areas including healthcare and robotics.
 The investment brings Pulumi’s total funding raised to $57.5 million. With the round, NEA partner Aaron Jacobson is joining the Pulumi board of directors, alongside existing board members S. Somasegar, managing director of Madrona Venture Group, and Sheila Gulati, managing director of Tola Capital.
 Pulumi says it will use the funds to accelerate its research and development, and expand its sales and marketing initiatives.  Jay Wampold, a veteran marketing executive with experience at companies including Chef, Amazon Web Services, Qumulo, and Cloudability, recently joined Pulumi as chief marketing officer. The company says it also hired  Lindsay Marolich as senior director of demand generation and  Kevin Kotecki as VP of sales.
 Other recent hires include  Lee-Ming Zen as VP of engineering, building out a technology team  led by CTO Luke Hoban.
  The company faces no shortage of competition. It’s going up against an array of high-profile alternatives in the field known as as infrastructure as code, including AWS CloudFormation, Hashicorp Terraform and others.
 Pulumi says its growth has accelerated this year, due in part to customers putting greater focus on new cloud projects amid the pandemic.
 “Clearly people are accelerating their move to the cloud,” said Rudder, Pulumi’s executive chairman and co-founder. “They’re prioritizing better in the face of a crisis. Projects that may have been on the backburner are now all of a sudden imperative to get to the cloud. That’s definitely accelerated the progress overall. And people are moving larger and larger portfolios to the cloud.”
 Pulumi lets its customers apply modern software development practices to what has traditionally been a cumbersome or manual process of provisioning, configuring and updating the underlying hardware and software for running applications. Its tools work across platforms and technologies including AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, Kubernetes and others, letting developers use general purpose languages including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python and Go, rather than learning specialized and single-purpose languages.
  Its customers range from small startups to large companies such as Mercedes-Benz, Tableau, DocuSign, VMware and Snowflake. Pulumi is based on an open-source model, with a community edition available for free to individual users. It sells multi-user and enterprise plans in a software-as-a-service model.
 The company says it’s now seeing more than 1 million downloads of its software tools each month, and overall adoption has increased 10x in the last year. More than 3,000 attendees registered for Pulumi’s Cloud Engineering Summit in October.
 “We’ve seen enormous growth, both in the open source community, but then also in the customer base,” said Duffy, the company’s co-founder and CEO, in an interview.
 Madrona Venture Group’s Somasegar said he sees two trends fueling Pulumi’s growth: an industry shift that has put the cloud at the center of software development; and the growing need for developers, operations specialists, security engineers and others to work together seamlessly in a continuous loop of design, development, deployment, monitoring and updates.
 “Those two secular trends are what I got excited about in the first place with Pulumi, and 3-and-a-half years later, those two trends are continuing to be super strong,” Somasegar said.

英国领先的通信提供商英国电信(BT)选择爱立信(Ericsson)在全国几个主要城市提供5G连接，包括所有四个首都伦敦、爱丁堡、贝尔法斯特和加的夫，这一举动备受期待。
就在一个月前，英国电信(BT)与诺基亚(Nokia)达成了一项类似的协议，这是英国政府将华为设备完全撤出英国5G网络的努力的一部分。此前，英国政府裁定，应禁止这家中国公司参与下一代移动连接的部署。
去年夏天，出于安全考虑，英国政府要求英国的CSP从2021年起停止从华为购买5G设备，并在未来7年内逐步停止在5G网络中使用华为的技术。目标是从2027年开始在没有华为设备的情况下运行5G网络。因此，诺基亚被选中在英国各地的BT无线电站点提供5G RAN(无线电接入网络)基础设施和服务。但由于英国电信历史上一直实行双供应商政策，以避免完全依赖单一供应商，预计很快就会宣布另一项合作伙伴关系。
鉴于爱立信与英国电信的良好关系，爱立信似乎是显而易见的选择。事实上，今年早些时候，英国电信(BT)选择爱立信(Ericsson)取代华为在核心5G网络中的设备，而核心5G网络是基础设施中更敏感的部分。
英国电信和爱立信之间的新协议扩大了两家公司之间的现有合作伙伴关系，一旦部署完成，爱立信将管理大约一半的英国电信5G流量。
爱立信总裁兼首席执行官伯杰·埃克霍尔姆(Börje Ekholm)表示：我们已经被选为5G Core的合作伙伴，我们很高兴通过这笔交易进一步加强合作关系，这笔交易将为英国各大城市的客户提供高性能和安全的5G服务，爱立信总裁兼首席执行官博杰·埃克霍尔姆(Börje Ekholm)表示。
该公司将部署5G RAN，并对之前的2G、3G和4G网络进行现代化改造，以在人口稠密和流量高的地点实现从4G到5G的迁移，利用一系列硬件、软件和服务，如天线系统、运输、电力和各种站点服务。
很大一部分工作将包括将目前在英国电信网络中使用的华为设备迁移到爱立信套件。任何由华为提供的、可以由爱立信升级到5G的4G设备，实际上都需要由这家瑞典公司提供的基础设施来更换。
这将是一个昂贵的过程，英国电信估计其整个网络将需要额外的5亿GB(6.3亿美元)，从技术角度来看也很困难。在不影响客户体验的情况下，将大量网络基础设施迁移到新供应商(无论是爱立信还是诺基亚)将是一项艰巨的挑战。
然而，英国电信有时间支持它。2027年的最后期限对该公司完成从华为的迁移来说是一个现实的时间框架。还有7年的时间，不太可能需要大规模的拆卸和更换--这种方法不可避免地会导致服务中断。
例如，在由多个小区站点覆盖的位置上，可以通过一次只关闭一个基站来逐步迁移设备。这将需要更长的时间，但可以避免服务中断。
CCS Insights的分析师凯斯特·曼(Kester Mann)告诉ZDNet：很明显，英国电信将需要一些时间来优先确保这一过程不会破坏客户体验。这很复杂，但有足够的时间来实现这一点。虽然有相关的成本，但我想说，提供的时间表对运营商来说是公平的。
就英国电信而言，它将政府的限制作为一个机会，从头开始重新思考该公司的网络，以适应新的人口结构和地理趋势。
英国电信(BT)首席技术和信息官霍华德·沃森(Howard Watson)在另一篇博客文章中表示，该公司与诺基亚(Nokia)和爱立信(Ericsson)的合作将使英国电信不仅能够遵守英国政府的要求，还将形成一个全新的网络基础设施，他称这是一个强大的机遇。
与此同时，华为发布了由华为赞助的研究公司Assembly的一份新报告，强调推迟在英国铺设5G将有可能在伦敦和东南部实现35万个新的就业机会，并危及1080亿GB(1400亿美元)的经济效益。
华为表示，将国家置于数字慢车道上，将扰乱制造业、机器人、医疗保健和远程学习等行业。
根据CCS Insight的曼恩的说法，禁止华为进入英国的网络基础设施也引发了人们对供应商多样性的担忧，因为它迫使CSP依赖有限数量的领先供应商--在这种情况下，主要供应商是诺基亚(Nokia)和爱立信(Ericsson)。
曼恩说，很难对华为的情况做出积极的评价。它总体上是负面的，而且对行业内的任何人都没有任何好处，包括客户。

In a widely-anticipated move, the UK's leading communications provider BT has picked Ericsson to provide 5G connectivity in several major cities across the country, including all four capitals London, Edinburgh, Belfast and Cardiff.
 The announcement comes just one month after BT  sealed a similar deal with Nokia, as part of an effort to remove Huawei equipment entirely from the country's 5G networks, following the UK government's ruling that the Chinese company should be barred from participating in the deployment of next-generation mobile connectivity.
    Last summer, security concerns prompted the government to require CSPs in the UK to  halt the purchase of 5G equipment from Huawei from 2021, and to phase out the use of the Chinese company's technology in 5G networks over the next seven years. The objective is to run 5G networks with no Huawei equipment whatsoever from 2027.   Nokia was therefore selected to provide 5G RAN (Radio Access Network) infrastructure and services at BT radio sites across the UK. But with BT historically operating a dual-vendor policy to avoid total dependence on a single provider, it was expected that another partnership announcement would soon follow.
 Ericsson appeared to be the obvious choice, given the Swedish company's well-established relationship with BT. In fact, earlier this year Ericsson was  selected by BT to replace Huawei's equipment in core 5G networks, which are more sensitive parts of the infrastructure.
 Expanding on the existing partnership between the two companies, the new agreement between BT and Ericsson will see the Swedish company managing around half of BT's 5G traffic once the deployment is completed.
 "Having already been selected to partner in 5G Core, we are pleased to strengthen the relationship further with this deal that will deliver high performance and secure 5G to their customers across the UK's major cities," said Börje Ekholm, president and CEO of Ericsson.
 The company will deploy 5G RAN and modernize previous 2G, 3G and 4G networks to enable migration from 4G to 5G in densely populated and high-traffic locations, tapping a portfolio of hardware, software and services such as antenna systems, transport, power, and various site services.
   A large part of the work will consist of migrating Huawei equipment that's currently used in BT's networks over to Ericsson kit. Any 4G equipment provided by Huawei, and which can be upgraded to 5G by Ericsson, will effectively need to be replaced by infrastructure provided by the Swedish company.
  This will be a costly process, which BT estimated will require an extra £500 million ($630 million) for its overall network, and it will also be difficult from a technical point of view. Migrating significant chunks of network infrastructure to a new vendor, be it Ericsson or Nokia, without affecting customers' experience, will be a tough challenge.
 BT has time on its side, however. The 2027 deadline is a realistic timeframe for the company to complete the migration away from Huawei. With seven years to go, it's unlikely that large-scale ripping and replacing will be necessary -- a method that would inevitably result in service disruption.
 For example, on a location covered by several cell sites, it will be possible to gradually migrate the equipment by only switching off one base station at a time. This would take longer, but avoid a service outage.
 Kester Mann, analyst at CCS Insights, told ZDNet: "It is obvious that BT will take some time to ensure as a priority that the process doesn't disrupt customer experience. It is complicated, but there is plenty of time for this to happen. Although there are costs associated, I would say that the timeframe provided is fair on operators."
 BT, for its part, is pitching the government restrictions as an opportunity to re-think the company's network from the ground up, to adapt to new demographics and geographic trends.
 In a separate blog post, Howard Watson, BT's chief technology and information officer, said that the company's partnerships with Nokia and Ericsson would enable BT to comply with the UK government's requirements, but also to shape a brand-new network infrastructure in what he called a " powerful opportunity".
 At the same time, Huawei has published a new report from research firm Assembly -- which the Chinese company sponsored -- highlighting that a delay in rolling out 5G in the UK would  risk the realisation of 350,000 new jobs in London and the South-East, and jeopardise £108 billion ($140 billion) of economic benefit.
 Putting the country on the digital slow lane, said Huawei, will disrupt industries including manufacturing, robotics, healthcare and remote learning.
 According to CCS Insight's Mann, banning Huawei from the UK's network infrastructure has also raised concerns about vendor diversity, by forcing CSPs to rely on a limited number of leading suppliers -- in this case, Nokia and Ericsson.
 "It's hard to put a positive spin on the Huawei situation," said Mann. "It's overall negative, and it doesn't do anyone in the industry any good at all, including customers."

谷歌母公司Alphabet透露，其一个Loon气球在高空飞行了312天，远远打破了之前223天的平流层飞行纪录。在此期间，它经历了一段相当长的旅程，在Peurto Rico推出，然后前往秘鲁，在那里提供了为期三个月的互联网服务测试。然后它环绕全球航行，经过南大西洋、印度洋和南太平洋，然后在墨西哥的巴哈被发现-大约10个月后，13.5万英里。
Alphabet表示，它通过设计、材料科学、制造和气球的飞行方式达到了里程碑。例如，该团队“在我们开始硬件之前就进行了数百万次模拟飞行，”Loon CTO塞尔瓦托·坎迪多(Luon CTO)塞尔瓦托·坎迪多(Salatore Candido)写道。
它现在也可以建造它们，这样就没有微小的缺陷可以大幅缩短飞行时间。坎迪多写道：“我们已经能够弄清楚如何可靠地、大规模地切割，缝合成南瓜形状，包装好，装在一个巨大的盒子里，然后把气球运到全国各地的发射场。”该公司还学会了如何在大约45分钟内装满气球，并在这样做的同时为它们遮风挡风，以确保它们在发射前不会受到天气的冲击。
当他们真的飞行时，谷歌监控“数百个与飞行系统性能相关的遥测数据流”，调整压舱物和其他参数，以最大化飞行时间。如果可能的话，所有的气球在着陆时都会被找回，然后使用“本质上是世界上最大的气球……”进行分析。平板扫描仪，“允许工程师对其进行分析和改进。
所有这些工作意味着Loon气球可以在高空停留更长时间，从而降低每小时的飞行成本。它还应该有助于更一致的连接-特别是如果像Alphabet希望的那样，将气球部署到世界各地的偏远地区提供互联网服务。
note_en:
Google parent Alphabet has  revealed that one of its Loon balloons spent 312 days aloft, breaking its  previous stratospheric flight record of 223 days, by a wide margin. It had quite a journey during that time, launching in Peurto Rico and heading to Peru, where it provided three months of test internet service. It then circumnavigated the entire globe, passing over the South Atlantic, Indian and South Pacific oceans, before being picked up in Baja, Mexico — some 10 months and 135,000 miles later.
  Alphabet said that it hit the milestone through a combination of design, materials science, manufacturing and how the balloons are flown. For instance, the team flies “millions of simulated flights before we even get started on the hardware,” wrote Loon CTO Salvatore Candido.
  It can also now build them so there are no tiny defects that can drastically shorten flights. “We’ve been able to figure out how to reliably and at scale cut, seam together in a pumpkin shape, wrap up, stuff in a giant box, and ship balloons across the country to our launch sites,” Candido wrote. The company also learned how to fill the balloons in around 45 minutes and shield them from wind while doing so, ensuring they don’t get battered by the elements before launching.
 And when they do fly, Google monitors “hundreds of telemetry data streams related to flight system performance,” adjusting ballast and other parameters to maximize flight time. All the balloons are recovered when they land, if possible, then analyzed using “what is essentially the world’s largest... flatbed scanner,” allowing engineers to analyze them and make improvements.
 All of that work means the Loon balloons can stay aloft longer, lowering flight costs per hour. It should also make for more consistent connectivity — especially if the balloons are deployed to provide internet service in remote areas around the world, as Alphabet hopes.
title: Alphabet‘s Loon用它的一个气球环绕地球飞行了312天，创造了平流层飞行最长时间的记录
title_en: Alphabet's Loon claims the record for longest stratospheric flight of 312 days while circumnavigating the globe, with one of its balloons
